project: Inspector Roofing Public Roof-Photo Label Taxonomy
source: Public-safe taxonomy derived from Inspector Roofing roof-inspection documentation workflows.
private_corpus_note: The production image corpus is private. This public taxonomy describes label meanings and safe educational use only.

classes.name: hail_hit
classes.definition: Localized roof surface mark or impact pattern that may be consistent with hail impact and requires human verification.
classes.safe_public_use: Explain why shingle marks should be reviewed alongside soft metals, slope context, weather history, and look-alike conditions.

classes.name: wind_crease
classes.plain_english_label: possible wind crease or uplift mark
classes.definition: Crease, uplift mark, or fold pattern on a shingle consistent with possible wind-related movement and requiring human verification.
classes.safe_public_use: Explain why lifted tabs, creases, missing shingles, seal condition, directionality, and age need to be documented carefully.

classes.name: soft_metal_impact
classes.definition: Visible dent or impact mark on soft metal components such as vents, gutters, flashing, caps, or roof accessories.
classes.safe_public_use: Explain why metal accessories can provide context when hail is being documented, without claiming coverage or causation.

classes.name: missing_shingle
classes.definition: Absent, torn, or displaced shingle area visible from inspection imagery.
classes.safe_public_use: Explain temporary protection, leak risk, repairability, and why missing material should be photographed from safe angles.

classes.name: granule_loss
classes.definition: Visible granule displacement or loss area requiring differentiation from age, foot traffic, blistering, manufacturing issues, or storm-related conditions.
classes.safe_public_use: Explain why age, wear, installation, traffic, and storm context need to be separated before making repair or replacement decisions.

classes.name: mechanical_damage
classes.definition: Damage pattern more consistent with tool marks, foot traffic, abrasion, installation handling, or non-weather mechanical action.
classes.safe_public_use: Explain why not every mark is storm damage and why careful classification protects homeowners from bad assumptions.

classes.name: non_damage_lookalike
classes.definition: Condition that may look like damage in photos but should not be treated as storm damage without human review.
classes.safe_public_use: Explain why inspection notes should identify look-alikes, uncertainty, and conditions that require closer review.

global_disclaimer: Labels support documentation, education, and model training. They do not determine insurance coverage, causation, repairability, code compliance, engineering conclusions, or claim approval.
